channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Energized House Republicans look to Trump for guidance at annual policy retreat
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump is expected to rally House Republicans Monday at their annual policy retreat as they look to make progress on tax cuts, border security and other conservative priorities.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal investigations into President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman gets more than 3-year prison term for helping pregnant Chinese women get to US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California woman was sentenced Monday to more than 3 years in prison in a long-running case over a business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who automatically became American citizens. U.S. District Judge R.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Snow briefly closes major interstate north of LA as needed rain douses California fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Snow shut down a key interstate north of Los Angeles for hours on Monday while weekend downpours doused wildfires across Southern California in the first significant storm of the season for the region that had not seen rainfall for eight months.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At Auschwitz memorial, survivors see echoes of the past in rising antisemitism
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — Auschwitz survivors warned Monday of the rising antisemitism and hatred they are witnessing in the modern world as they gathered with world leaders and European royalty on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KREX 5

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately
item.description: U.S. public health officials have been told to stop working with the World Health Organization, effective immediately.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nigerian extradited to US to face charges over nude photo that led to teen's death
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A Nigerian man has been extradited to the U.S. and charged with causing the death of a South Carolina teen who took his own life after the suspect posed as a woman and tried to extort the teen after he sent nude photos, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pardoned Jan. 6 rioter killed by Indiana deputy while resisting arrest: State Police
item.description: Matthew Huttle, 42, of Hobart was shot and killed during a traffic stop near the Jasper/Pulaski county lines on Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, KRON4

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KRON4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Missing 12-year-old child's mom reveals he never made it to friend's house
item.description: As Virginia Beach Police have deployed a massive search for Juan Sebastian Meija Acevedo, the mother of the missing 12-year-old told her side to WAVY 10. She revealed to 10 On Your Side that her son never made it to his friend's house.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, NewsNation

item.title: Tax season begins Monday: How to file taxes online for free if you made $84K or less
item.description: There are programs available to help people who made $84K or less last year file their federal taxes for free.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Clarke, Local 4

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fewer women are getting tested for cancer: Survey
item.description: The Hologic Women's Health Index's 2023 data found a decrease in testing for any cancer among women — a first in the survey's four-year history.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: ICE conducts 'enhanced targeted operations' in Austin
item.description: A series of ICE raids were conducted in Austin on Sunday, according to sources from the Drug Enforcement Administration.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Penske's pair of Porsches dominate as The Captain wins Rolex 24 at Daytona for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Roger Penske started his 59th season in motorsports the same way he closed last year — as a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: Top Trump administration officials visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcement in the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: Rain has fallen around Southern California and is expected to aid firefighters mopping up multiple wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KRON4

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, KRON4

item.title: Tattoo removal expert says 1 in 7 clients asks to remove this specific tattoo
item.description: Today’s tattoo-removal experts are quite adept at ridding their clients of all types of ill-conceived tattoos. But there are a few specific categories of ill-conceived ink that they tend to treat more often than others.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump’s first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth’s climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: London court clears way to extradite US mother accused of killing 2 of her children in Colorado
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A London judge on Friday rejected a U.S. mother's challenge to her extradition to face murder charges in Colorado in the deaths of two of her young children.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman finds police-issued rifle, bulletproof vests in Hertz rental
item.description: A woman was shocked after she opened the trunk of her rental car to find a police-issued assault rifle.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, Fox 5

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4
